page: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core with hyperthreading enabled, yielding 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of system memory, translating to a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for deep learning operations. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong single-core capabilities and constrained multi-core scaling. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded tasks, placing in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core performance and single-threaded gzip compression. However, because the 2 vCPUs share a single physical core, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench multi-core scoring in the bottom 25%. Memory performance also presents a tradeoff; while capacity is high, uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks are positioned in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX2ms_v2 offers a high density of memory relative to its vCPU count. This resource profile is advantageous for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand highly parallelized compute resources, preventing the need to provision excess vCPUs solely to meet memory requirements. The instance is suited for single-threaded data processing, memory-bound applications with low concurrency, lightweight database operations, and text processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks demonstrate a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. The instance delivers top-tier single-threaded performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and gzip compression. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are weak, reflecting the physical single-core limitation. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, low-concurrency workloads, making it suitable for single-threaded data processing, text processing, and memory-bound applications that do not require extensive parallel compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 42 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance paired with limited multi-core scaling.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and high memory density for low-concurrency, memory-bound workloads.

42.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core memory density
Top-tier single-core performance in gzip compression and Geekbench
Limited multi-core scaling due to single physical core architecture
No local storage bundled, with support for Premium Storage
Suited for single-threaded data processing and memory-bound tasks
